description: Letter from Isma’il b. Farah from Alexandria, to Nahray b. Nissim, Fustat. Around 1060. The writer deals with selling goods that arrived from the Maghreb. Mentions details about the eastern wind that delays the ships on their way from the Maghreb to Egypt. Mentions details about Byzantine merchants and the prices of several types of goods: resin, fabrics, soap, and oil. Also mentions a shipment of wax, that has not been sold entirely yet. (Information from Gil, Kingdom, Vol. 3, #498) VMR
description_he: מכתב מישמעאל בן פרח, אלכסנדריה, לנהראי בן ניסים, פוסטאט, כ-1060, על מכירת סחורות מהמגרב וסוחרים ביזנטים ומחירים
